parks.description: Acadia combines rocky Atlantic shoreline, mountain summits, and carriage roads for hiking, biking, and scenic drives.
parks.imageAlt: A brilliant sunset filled with hues of blue, red, orange, magenta, and purple highlight the sky.

parks.description: Arches protects a high desert landscape filled with sandstone arches, balanced rocks, and dramatic red formations.
parks.imageAlt: A crowd of people sit and watch the sunset at delicate arch.

parks.description: Badlands features layered buttes and canyons alongside mixed-grass prairie that supports bison and bighorn sheep.
parks.imageAlt: Layered badlands formations behind fields of green grass under cloudy and billowing clouds.

parks.description: Bryce Canyon is known for hoodoos, high-elevation viewpoints, and trails winding through colorful amphitheaters.
parks.imageAlt: A red rock landscape and plateau forest glows with the morning sun.

parks.description: Canyonlands offers vast mesas, deep river canyons, and remote landscapes shaped by the Colorado and Green Rivers.
parks.imageAlt: A broad stone arch with rock pinnacles in the distance.

parks.description: Cuyahoga Valley preserves forests, rolling hills, and the Cuyahoga River with trails, waterfalls, and rail access.
parks.imageAlt: People in bright orange kayaks paddle around a bend in a river, past green trees and a rocky shore.

parks.description: Everglades protects subtropical wetlands with mangroves, sawgrass marshes, and habitat for unique wildlife.
parks.imageAlt: A sunset creates a silhouette of a cypress tree with needle-like leaves shaped like an N.

parks.description: Glacier includes alpine lakes, rugged peaks, and iconic drives through mountain scenery and historic landscapes.
parks.imageAlt: Large mountains dotted with snow loom above a rocky meadow filled with yellow flowers.

parks.description: Grand Canyon reveals immense geologic layers, river corridors, and viewpoints that span one of the world's great landscapes.
parks.imageAlt: The canyon glows orange as people visit Mather Point, a rock outcropping that juts into Grand Canyon.

parks.description: Grand Teton features jagged mountains, glacial lakes, and valley ecosystems with access to iconic western vistas.
parks.imageAlt: Backpackers in North Fork Cascade Canyon with Grand Teton rising behind them.

parks.description: Olympic contains temperate rain forests, mountain terrain, and Pacific coastline across diverse ecosystems.
parks.imageAlt: A herd of elk crossing a river.

parks.description: Rocky Mountain offers high-alpine trails, tundra ecosystems, and panoramic views along Trail Ridge Road.
parks.imageAlt: Rocky Mountain National Park sign on a sunny day with a clear sky overhead.

parks.fullName: Sequoia and Kings Canyon National Parks
parks.description: These neighboring parks protect giant sequoias, deep canyons, and high Sierra landscapes.
parks.imageAlt: A deep canyon with a forested floor and steep granite cliffs.

parks.description: Yellowstone is known for geysers, hot springs, wildlife, and geothermal basins spread across a vast plateau.
parks.imageAlt: Brilliant blues and greens of a hot spring ringed by oranges, yellows, reds, and browns.

parks.description: Yosemite features granite cliffs, waterfalls, giant sequoias, and valley meadows with year-round recreation.
parks.imageAlt: Two tall waterfalls flowing down snow covered granite walls.
